summary: Smoke-tested new transcript drill-down + portfolio image perf pass. Backend endpoints all green (auth gating, 404, draft merge into lead, transcript sorted asc, regression CSV/bookings). Admin UI transcript expand/collapse works with all captured fields + chat bubbles + role labels + timestamps. Portfolio images use <picture> with avif+webp sources, multi-width srcset, explicit dimensions, eager+fetchpriority high on first card, lazy on later cards. One React dev warning about fetchpriority casing flagged.
passed: GET /api/admin/leads/unknown/chat with valid token -> 404 {detail:'Lead not found'}
Chat-draft service/budget merged into lead on email arrival (lead has service='AI Chatbots', budget='AED 12000', email='flow@apex.media', phone='+971500001234', source='apex_ai_chat')
Transcript endpoint returns lead+transcript(2 msgs user+assistant)+draft=null; messages have role/content/created_at; sorted ascending
Admin login at /admin with token apex-admin-2026 renders 32-row leads table

Clicking [data-testid=expand-{id}] reveals [data-testid=transcript-{id}] with 'CAPTURED DETAILS' (Name/Email/Phone/Service/Budget/Note) + 'APEX AI TRANSCRIPT · 2 MESSAGES' showing VISITOR / APEX AI bubbles with timestamps
Second click collapses the transcript row
Portfolio renders with image/avif + image/webp , srcset contains width descriptors (480w...), has explicit width=1400 height=900
First portfolio card img has loading='eager' and fetchpriority='high'; cards idx>=2 have loading='lazy'

failed.issue: React dev console warning: 'Invalid DOM property fetchpriority. Did you mean fetchPriority?' (the attribute does end up on the DOM, but React 18 prefers camelCase prop name)
failed.evidence: console.error captured on / -> 'Invalid DOM property `%s`. Did you mean `%s`? fetchpriority fetchPriority'

failed.issue: Second portfolio card (idx=1) has loading='eager' fetchpriority='low' instead of pure loading='lazy' as spec describes 'subsequent cards loading=lazy'. Card idx>=2 are correctly lazy.

action_items: Optional: rename JSX attribute from fetchpriority to fetchPriority in Portfolio.jsx (and index.html preload uses lower-case which is fine in HTML) to silence React warning
Optional: gate loading='eager' to only the first card (idx===0) for stricter lazy-loading
